title: Real-Time Patterns Quiz
description: Test your understanding of real-time communication patterns.

questions.explanation: All three are valid real-time patterns. Long polling maintains an open HTTP request that the server responds to when data is available. Server-Sent Events use a persistent HTTP connection for server-to-client streaming. WebSockets provide bidirectional communication over a persistent connection.
questions.question: Which of the following are valid real-time communication patterns?

options: Polling is more efficient than WebSockets
Server-Sent Events require a special server protocol
Long polling cannot handle high-frequency updates
questions.explanation: WebSockets maintain a persistent connection that allows both client and server to send messages at any time (bidirectional). This is more efficient than polling for real-time applications.
questions.question: What is a key advantage of WebSockets over HTTP polling?

options: When you need real-time bidirectional communication
When you need to support very old browsers
questions.explanation: WebSockets are ideal when you need real-time bidirectional communication like chat, multiplayer games, or collaborative editing. For simple streaming, Server-Sent Events might be simpler.
questions.question: When should you choose WebSockets over other patterns?

options: Choose based on the specific requirements and trade-offs
Never use polling in modern applications
Server-Sent Events are always the best choice
questions.explanation: Each pattern has trade-offs. WebSockets add complexity but offer the best real-time performance. Polling is simpler but less efficient. Server-Sent Events are great for one-way streaming. Choose based on your specific needs.
questions.question: How should you choose between real-time patterns?
